data-local-id="70dd9ef24b5b">Notes: row wise GBIX definitions are newly designed. Column wise are dev definition. Green highlights are agreements and red highlights are disagreements. Majority of the disagreements are on 61+DPD ever, 31+DPD at outcome point (newly introduced) and ever in arrears. The cause of the disagreements are mostly due to extension of the performance window from rolling 9 months to up-to-current. After review, we are comfortable to adopt the new GBIX definitions.</p><h3 id="Final-GBIX-over-sample-months" data-local-id="70f5d32ad493" data-renderer-start-pos="2524" tabindex="-1">Final GBIX over applicant ages is different: ZM goes up to 70 and ZP only goes up to 60.</em></p></li><li><p data-renderer-start-pos="5229" data-local-id="57dd45b4c9e9"><em data-renderer-mark="true">Submission times are grouped into night vs non-night. For ZM, night is between 0AM and 6:59AM while for ZP night is between 0AM and 4:59AM.</em></p></li></ol><h2 id="The-key-findings" data-local-id="ff932bb4db4f" data-renderer-start-pos="5372" We found some applications whose age of file is 0 but have enquires. To remediate this data inaccuracy, we impute the age of file using the enquiry data: use the oldest enquiry’s date to derive the age of file. Notice that this is not an accurate way to impute age of file. But it is better than leaving the data as it is.</p><h2 id="Single-factor-analysis" data-local-id="8b7e09ffcd89" data-renderer-start-pos="6194" tabindex="-1">Single factor analysis<span
